topicDefinition.oneLiner: Discover the top AI video generators that turn text into professional videos.
boundaries: Must have native AI video generation (text-to-video, image-to-video, or avatar generation).
Excludes traditional video editors that only use AI for peripheral tasks (like auto-captions) without generating the core video.

howToChoose.title: Evaluate Your Core Use Case
howToChoose.desc: Are you creating cinematic B-roll (Sora, Veo, Runway), talking-head presentations (Synthesia, HeyGen), or viral social media clips (Pika, Luma)?

howToChoose.title: Check Cost and Credit Limits
howToChoose.desc: Most platforms use a credit system. Verify how many seconds or minutes of video you actually get per month on standard paid tiers.

howToChoose.title: Verify Commercial Licensing
howToChoose.desc: If you are an agency or marketer, ensure the tier you purchase grants full commercial rights to the generated outputs.

howToChoose.title: Consider Output Resolution and Watermarks
howToChoose.desc: Check if high-definition (1080p or 4K) exports and watermark removal require an expensive Pro or Enterprise upgrade.

sources.title: 14 Best AI Video Generators 2026 - Complete Guide
sources.excerpt: A comprehensive breakdown of the top AI video generation tools categorized by cinematic quality, avatars, and social media speed.

sections.heading: Top-Tier Tools for Cinematic Quality
sections.content: OpenAI Sora 2 leads for photorealistic text-to-video generation, offering 15-second clips with excellent audio and video quality. Google Veo 3.1 follows closely for fidelity. Runway (Gen-series) excels for creative control.

sections.heading: For avatars and training
sections.content: Synthesia and HeyGen (Avatar IV) support multi-language content, with HeyGen offering 175+ languages and voice cloning.

keyClaims: Offers 15-second clips (25 seconds with Pro storyboard mode)
limitations: Commercial licensing typically requires paid subscriptions
proofSnippets: OpenAI Sora 2 leads for photorealistic text-to-video generation, offering 15-second clips (25 seconds with Pro storyboard mode) with excellent audio and video quality, though outputs include watermarks.
Synthesia and HeyGen (Avatar IV) support multi-language content
Pika (Model 2.2) generates ultra-fast, effects-driven content for TikTok and Reels.

notes: Focused heavily on high-end generative models for 2026.

sources.title: HeyGen vs Synthesia Pricing: Full Cost Breakdown
sources.excerpt: A detailed comparison of pricing models, limits, and credit systems for top avatar video generators.

sections.heading: Pricing Overview
sections.content: HeyGen offers three tiers: Free ($0/mo), Creator ($29/mo), Pro ($99/mo). Synthesia offers Basic (Free), Starter ($29/mo), Creator ($89/mo), and Enterprise.

sections.heading: Watermarks and Limits
sections.content: HeyGen's Creator plan ($29/mo) includes watermark removal. Synthesia includes watermark removal in the Starter plan ($29/mo).

keyClaims: Uses a consumption-based model with credit add-ons
limitations: Creator plan caps Avatar IV models to ~10 minutes per month
proofSnippets: HeyGen's 'unlimited' Creator plan actually caps high-quality Avatar IV models to approximately 10 minutes per month on the base tier.

keyClaims: Operates on a seat-based governance model with predictable costs
watermarkRules: Watermark removal is included in the Starter plan ($29/mo) and higher
limitations: Custom pricing required for SCORM export and SSO
proofSnippets: Synthesia uses a credit system with hard caps: Starter gets 10 minutes/month, Creator gets 30 minutes/month.

keyClaims: Excels for creative control across various plan options
proofSnippets: Runway starts at $15/mo with 625 credits for creative effects and short clips.

notes: Excellent details on exact limits which are usually hidden behind marketing copy.
